key_insight: The paper turns lunar landing trajectory optimization into a hard-constrained neural network problem by embedding Pontryagin optimality conditions and learning only the remaining state-costate-value components.
engineering_relevance: This offers a real-time, fixed-cost guidance approach for energy-optimal lunar powered descent from varying initial conditions without solving a boundary-value problem onboard.
method: Hard-encodes boundary and transversality conditions in the network architecture.
Uses the closed-form Pontryagin-optimal thrust magnitude and direction directly, rather than learning the control law.
Trains on physics-residual losses from optimality conditions only, with no precomputed optimal trajectories.
evidence_signal: The trained policy was tested without retraining on six representative initial states and 80 Monte Carlo runs, showing close agreement with an independently solved indirect-method benchmark and small residuals.
watch_item: The results are still preliminary and depend on the quality of offline training and the validity of the assumed bounded operating envelope.
paper_title: Optimality-Informed Neural Networks for Lunar Landing Trajectory Optimization
